content:
Summary: Fixed terminal tab numbering so labels no longer reuse closed tab numbers. The workspace now persists a nextTabNumber counter and derives a safe fallback from existing Terminal N labels when restoring older saved state.

Facts:
1. [finished] frontend/src/features/terminal/TerminalWorkspace.tsx now stores nextTabNumber in terminal workspace state and persists it to localStorage alongside activeTabId and tabs.
2. [finished] When restoring older terminal workspace state without nextTabNumber, the code derives the next number from the highest existing label that matches the 'Terminal N' pattern.
3. [finished] Adding a new terminal tab now uses currentState.nextTabNumber instead of tabs.length + 1, so closed tabs do not cause duplicate labels like Terminal 4 appearing twice.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\terminal\TerminalWorkspace.tsx — Added persistent nextTabNumber handling and label derivation logic so newly opened tabs never reuse closed tab labels. | Notes: Updated default state, restore path, add-tab path, and close-tab path.

content:
Summary: Implemented terminal interaction UX improvements so right-click no longer opens the browser context menu, right-click now copies selected terminal text, and paste now uses a controlled plain-text path that clears selection to avoid lingering highlight artifacts.

Facts:
1. [finished] TerminalPane now disables xterm right-click word selection and registers a capture-phase contextmenu handler on the terminal container that prevents default browser menu behavior and copies selected terminal text to clipboard.
2. [finished] TerminalPane now registers a capture-phase paste handler that prevents default browser paste behavior, reads plain text from clipboard data, clears terminal selection, pastes into xterm, and refocuses the terminal.
3. [finished] Clipboard copy behavior uses navigator.clipboard.writeText when available with a fallback textarea + document.execCommand copy path to support restricted clipboard permission contexts.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\terminal\TerminalPane.tsx — Added right-click copy and controlled paste handlers, clipboard helper functions, and disabled xterm right-click word selection to fix terminal UX issues. | Notes: Context menu is suppressed in terminal; paste now clears selection and uses plain text clipboard data.
2. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\terminal\TerminalPane.tsx — Reviewed existing terminal lifecycle and input wiring to ensure event-handler changes stayed scoped to presentation/input responsibility. | Notes: No runtime/session API changes required.

content:
Summary: Fixed the routes page table border clipping issue and then restored dropdown overlay behavior by portaling the custom dropdown menu to document.body with viewport-based positioning. The table card keeps rounded borders clipped correctly while the 'All accounts' dropdown now renders above the card and surrounding content. Validation passed with targeted Biome lint on the touched files.

Facts:
1. [finished] The routes table card border issue was caused by the outer container needing overflow hidden to preserve rounded corners, which also affected nested overlay behavior.
2. [finished] The custom dropdown component now renders its menu in a portal attached to document.body and measures its trigger position to place the menu correctly in the viewport.
3. [finished] Targeted lint validation passed for src/components/CustomDropdown.tsx and src/features/routes/RouteTable.tsx after the fix.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\RouteTable.tsx — Changed the table card overflow behavior to preserve rounded border clipping on the routes page. | Notes: Set overflow to hidden on the card wrapper.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\components\CustomDropdown.tsx — Moved the dropdown menu into a portal and added viewport-based positioning so the menu renders above clipped containers. | Notes: Also updated outside-click handling to include the portaled menu.

The table card keeps rounded borders clipped correctly while the 'All accounts' dropdown now renders above the card and surrounding content. Validation passed with targeted Biome lint on the touched files.

content:
Summary: Fixed the frontend production build failure caused by the new /api/events route. The issue was an invalid relative import path to backendProxy; changing it from '../../../../server/backendProxy' to '../../../server/backendProxy' allowed Next.js production build to succeed. This was a narrow build-only correction after the earlier realtime refresh feature landed.

Facts:
1. [finished] The /api/events frontend route lives at frontend/src/app/api/events/route.ts and must import backendProxy from frontend/src/server/backendProxy.ts using '../../../server/backendProxy'.
2. [finished] The frontend production build now succeeds after fixing the import path in /api/events/route.ts.
3. [finished] The realtime refresh feature remains implemented with SSE, backend hub publishing, and page subscriptions; this follow-up only fixed the build breakage.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/api/events/route.ts — Corrected the relative import so Next.js can resolve backendProxy during production build of the app route. | Notes: Changed '../../../../server/backendProxy' to '../../../server/backendProxy'.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/server/backendProxy.ts — Confirmed the target module path and existing proxy helper used by the new events route. | Notes: No code changes were needed here.

metadata.summary: Fixed the frontend production build failure caused by the new /api/events route. The issue was an invalid relative import path to backendProxy; changing it from '../../../../server/backendProxy' to '../../../server/backendProxy' allowed Next.js production build to succeed. This was a narrow build-only correction after the earlier realtime refresh feature landed.

content:
Summary: Implemented SSE-based realtime refresh for routes and admin users pages so open clients refetch when routes or users change on another device. Added a reusable frontend EventSource hook, a proxy route for the event stream, a backend realtime hub, and backend publish calls on route/user mutations. Verified backend api/realtime tests pass; frontend targeted lint still reports a formatting issue in the large RoutesWorkspace file, and the full frontend lint has unrelated pre-existing violations elsewhere in the repo.

Facts:
1. [finished] The backend now exposes a realtime event stream at /api/events and publishes route/user change events through an in-memory hub.
2. [finished] The frontend now listens to the realtime stream from the routes and admin users workspaces and silently refetches when matching events arrive.
3. [finished] A new frontend proxy route forwards /api/events to the backend so EventSource can connect through the app origin with cookies intact.
4. [finished] Backend targeted tests for internal/api and internal/realtime passed after the realtime change; the broader backend test run still contains an unrelated pre-existing registry failure.
5. [finished] Frontend targeted Biome lint still reports a formatting issue in frontend/src/features/routes/RoutesWorkspace.tsx after the change, while the full frontend lint also reports unrelated existing repository issues.

Affected files:
1. [created] backend/internal/realtime/hub.go — Implements the backend in-memory publish/subscribe hub that stores and broadcasts route and user change events to connected clients. | Notes: New realtime transport primitive for SSE.
2. [created] backend/internal/realtime/hub_test.go — Covers the realtime hub broadcast and unsubscribe behavior so the push mechanism is safe to use in the backend. | Notes: Unit tests for publish/subscribe semantics.
3. [modified] backend/internal/api/handler.go — Adds the /api/events SSE endpoint and publishes route/user change events after successful mutations. | Notes: Main API orchestration now emits realtime notifications.
4. [modified] backend/cmd/routegate/main.go — Constructs and injects the realtime hub into the API handler at startup. | Notes: Wires the new transport into the backend entrypoint.
5. [modified] frontend/src/server/backendProxy.ts — Preserves streaming response headers like x-accel-buffering for the EventSource proxy path. | Notes: Needed so the frontend proxy can relay SSE correctly.
6. [created] frontend/src/app/api/events/route.ts — Forwards the browser-facing /api/events endpoint to the backend SSE stream while keeping auth cookies on the same origin. | Notes: New proxy route for realtime subscriptions.
7. [created] frontend/src/features/realtime/useRealtimeRefresh.ts — Provides a reusable client-side EventSource subscription hook that refetches on matching realtime events. | Notes: Shared hook used by routes and users workspaces.
8. [modified] frontend/src/features/routes/RoutesWorkspace.tsx — Subscribes the routes workspace to route change events so the list silently refetches when another client creates or edits routes. | Notes: This file is still flagged by Biome formatting in targeted lint.
9. [modified] frontend/src/features/users/UsersWorkspace.tsx — Subscribes the admin users workspace to user change events so the admin list silently refetches when another client creates or edits users. | Notes: Realtime refresh for admin account changes.

Added a reusable frontend EventSource hook, a proxy route for the event stream, a backend realtime hub, and backend publish calls on route/user mutations. Verified backend api/realtime tests pass; frontend targeted lint still reports a formatting issue in the large RoutesWorkspace file, and the full frontend lint has unrelated pre-existing violations elsewhere in the repo.

content:
Summary: Fixed the remaining divider inset in the settings right panel by removing desktop padding from the container and moving it to the body only. The header/divider strip now spans edge-to-edge, while the content body keeps the interior padding separately on mobile and desktop.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user identified that the remaining left/right gap around the live divider was caused by padding on the right-panel container, especially the desktop `1.15rem` padding.
2. [finished] `frontend/src/app/globals.css` now keeps `.settings-content` padding at 0 and applies the desktop `1.15rem` spacing to `.settings-content__body` instead.
3. [finished] The right-panel header divider remains full width because it is attached to the unpadded header strip, while the panel body handles the inner spacing.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Moved the desktop padding off the right-panel container and onto the body so the header divider can span full width without gaps. | Notes: The header strip is now separate from content padding on desktop.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the shell markup already separates header and body, enabling the CSS-only fix. | Notes: No structural change was needed.

content:
Summary: Added a subtitle to the right-panel settings header and fixed the divider to span the full width without inset gaps. The settings shell now uses a dedicated full-width header row with title and subtitle, and the content body is padded separately so the divider aligns edge-to-edge cleanly.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user wanted the right-panel settings header to include a subtitle and for the divider to span the full panel width without left/right gaps.
2. [finished] `frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx` now renders a title plus subtitle in the right-panel header and wraps the active section content in a separate body container.
3. [finished] `frontend/src/app/globals.css` now gives the right-panel header full-width layout responsibility while the padding lives on the body container, which removes the divider inset gap.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Added subtitle text for the right-panel header and wrapped the active panel content in a separate body container. | Notes: The shell now owns the single header hierarchy.
2. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Refactored the settings content shell so the header spans full width and the body handles internal padding, eliminating divider gaps. | Notes: The header now has title and subtitle styles plus a separate body wrapper.

content:
Summary: Removed duplicate titles in the settings right panel by keeping the shell-level title and deleting the repeated per-section headers from the General and Terminal panels. The right panel now shows a single title hierarchy, with the shell header acting as the only section heading.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user noticed duplicate titles in the right settings panel and asked to keep the better single title.
2. [finished] The duplicate `General` and `Terminal` section headers were removed from `frontend/src/features/settings/GeneralSettingsPanel.tsx` and `frontend/src/features/settings/TerminalSettingsPanel.tsx`.
3. [finished] The shell-level title in `frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx` remains the visible title for the right panel.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/features/settings/GeneralSettingsPanel.tsx — Removed the inner General section header so the right panel no longer repeats the title hierarchy. | Notes: The form content now starts immediately under the shell title.
2. [modified] frontend/src/features/settings/TerminalSettingsPanel.tsx — Removed the inner Terminal section header so the right panel no longer repeats the title hierarchy. | Notes: The form content now starts immediately under the shell title.
3. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Deleted no-longer-used section header styles after removing the duplicate panel headings. | Notes: This keeps the shared settings stylesheet aligned with the new single-title layout.
4. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the shell-level title is the one remaining section heading in the right panel. | Notes: No markup change was needed in the workspace shell.

content:
Summary: Tightened the right-side settings panel header so it no longer looks overly tall, and made its divider span the full width of the content area. The settings content header now uses slimmer vertical padding, a smaller title size, and negative horizontal margins so the bottom divider bleeds edge-to-edge.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user reported the General Settings and Terminal Settings header in the right panel was too tall and that its bottom divider did not span the full width.
2. [finished] The right-panel header styling now uses a slimmer title bar in `frontend/src/app/globals.css` with reduced padding and a smaller title size.
3. [finished] The bottom divider for the right-panel header now spans full width by using negative horizontal margins on `.settings-content__header` while keeping the content panel padding intact.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Adjusted the settings content header spacing, title sizing, and divider bleed to fix the tall header and full-width divider issue. | Notes: The header is slimmer and edge-to-edge.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the markup around the right-panel header so the CSS-only fix could be targeted safely. | Notes: No structural change was required.

content:
Summary: Fixed the settings sidebar border overlap so the General and Terminal separators render as single thin lines instead of doubled/thick borders. The sidebar now relies on the nav top border plus each row's bottom border, with the per-item top border removed to prevent overlap.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user reported that the General and Terminal top borders looked doubled or too thick in the settings sidebar.
2. [finished] The overlapping border issue was caused by border-top and border-bottom both being applied to the sidebar items in `frontend/src/app/globals.css`.
3. [finished] The current solution keeps the nav top border and each item's bottom border, while removing the item top border so the separator renders once.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Removed the item top border in the settings sidebar so adjacent rows no longer render doubled separators. | Notes: The nav retains a single top border and each item keeps its bottom border.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the sidebar structure that the CSS selectors target, ensuring the fix stayed isolated to styling. | Notes: No markup changes were required.

content:
Summary: Adjusted the settings sidebar separators to be lighter and thinner. The sidebar rows now use 0.5px neutral top and bottom borders, and the active row no longer introduces a darker border color, keeping the treatment soft and non-black.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user asked for the settings sidebar borders to avoid black-looking divider treatment and to make the top and bottom borders thinner.
2. [finished] The current implementation uses 0.5px neutral borders for the sidebar navigation rows and does not apply a darker active border color.
3. [finished] The work remains localized to `frontend/src/app/globals.css` and the existing settings shell composition was not changed in this pass.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Refined the settings sidebar row separators to be thinner and lighter, removing the dark active border treatment. | Notes: Adjusted nav and row border thickness to 0.5px and kept the active state neutral.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the layout shell remained stable while only the separator styling changed. | Notes: No markup changes were needed.

content:
Summary: Refined the RouteGate settings shell to keep the panel height stable and the sidebar visually clean. The settings workspace now uses a fixed-height shell with scrollable content, a white sidebar with a full-height right border, and row-separated navigation items with bottom borders and no left active marker.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user wanted the settings shell to stop resizing when switching between settings sections.
2. [finished] The settings sidebar is now white, uses a full-height right border as the main separator, and each navigation item keeps a bottom border.
3. [finished] The active sidebar item no longer uses a left-side marker; it is indicated by weight and border color instead.
4. [finished] The fixed-height behavior is implemented in `frontend/src/app/globals.css` by constraining `.settings-workspace` and making `.settings-content` scroll internally.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Updated the settings shell layout, sidebar border treatment, active state styling, and fixed-height behavior. | Notes: The workspace is fixed-height and the content area scrolls internally.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Confirmed the shell composition still cleanly separates the sidebar from the active settings panel. | Notes: No markup change was required for this pass.

content:
Summary: Refined the RouteGate settings sidebar so its navigation buttons are flush, full-width rows inside the panel rather than card-like pills. The sidebar now uses a full-bleed list with no internal gaps, and the active state is an inset accent instead of a separate bordered chip.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user wanted the settings side panel buttons to be full width, not card-like, with no top/bottom spacing gaps and no clipped-looking edges.
2. [finished] The affected files are `frontend/src/app/globals.css` for the sidebar row styling and `frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx` for the surrounding shell composition from the earlier pass.
3. [finished] The updated sidebar buttons now render as flush rows with zero vertical gap, full-width padding, and an inset active indicator instead of separate rounded button cards.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Reworked the settings sidebar layout so buttons are flush, full-width rows with no extra gaps and no card styling. | Notes: Added full-bleed nav rows, removed rounded button cards, and switched the active treatment to an inset accent.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the shell markup that wraps the sidebar and main content so the CSS change could stay localized. | Notes: No additional markup change was needed in this pass.

content:
Summary: Unified the settings sidebar and main content into a single connected shell in the RouteGate frontend. The settings workspace now uses one shared card container with an internal divider and full-width overflow clipping so the panels visually read as one integrated layout rather than separate cards.

Facts:
1. [finished] The user requested that the RouteGate side panel and main panel feel like one unified layout instead of two separated blocks.
2. [finished] The settings workspace is the affected shell: `frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx` renders the sidebar and content panel, and `frontend/src/app/globals.css` defines the shared layout styles.
3. [finished] The settings workspace now wraps both panels in a single `card` container, with the sidebar using a subtle tinted background and the content area using the primary surface.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Adjusted the settings shell markup so the sidebar and content area share one card container instead of separate cards. | Notes: Removed per-panel card styling and kept the section content intact.
2. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Updated the settings workspace styles to remove the visual gap between panels and add an internal divider for the unified shell. | Notes: Made the sidebar/content relationship feel connected while preserving responsiveness.

content:
Summary: Investigated a Routes admin-page name mismatch where admin saw WAH4H/WAH4P drift. Root cause was a denormalized route.ownerName snapshot in MongoDB; the backend Routes list previously returned the stored snapshot directly. I changed the registry list path to re-resolve current owner names from the users collection on read, with stored ownerName as fallback. Added a unit test for owner-name fallback and documented the behavior in the backend README. Targeted backend tests for registry and api passed; full `go test ./...` still has a separate pre-existing registry test failure in destination tests.

Facts:
1. [finished] Routes store an ownerName snapshot in MongoDB, but the list endpoint now resolves current owner names from the users collection before returning results.
2. [finished] The stale-name bug was caused by read-path denormalization drift, not by frontend caching; the frontend already requests routes with `cache: no-store`.
3. [finished] Backend files changed for this fix were `backend/internal/registry/store.go`, `backend/internal/registry/store_test.go`, and `backend/README.md`.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/internal/registry/store.go — Added user-based owner-name resolution in the route list path and preserved snapshot fallback logic. | Notes: Introduced a users collection reference, owner-name lookup helper, and display-name override conversion.
2. [modified] backend/internal/registry/store_test.go — Added coverage for the owner-name override/fallback behavior used by route listing. | Notes: Pure unit test for the public conversion helper.
3. [modified] backend/README.md — Documented that route list responses resolve current owner names from the users collection. | Notes: Clarifies denormalized snapshot behavior for future maintainers.

content:
Summary: Restructured the router maintenance page centering after repeated right-alignment complaints. The template now uses a dedicated fixed .viewport wrapper with inset 0 and grid place-items center, while main is a bounded centered column and heading lines center independently. This keeps 'is temporarily unavailable.' on one line without letting it shift the whole screen right.

Facts:
1. [finished] The router maintenance page now includes a fixed .viewport wrapper around main to own full-screen centering independently of body layout quirks.
2. [finished] The maintenance heading is split into two explicit lines: 'This destination' and 'is temporarily unavailable.'.
3. [finished] Each heading line uses width:max-content, max-width:100%, margin-inline:auto, and text-align:center so a nowrap line centers independently.
4. [finished] The router test suite passed after the maintenance page viewport restructure.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\router\internal\proxy\maintenance_page.go — Restructured maintenance page HTML and CSS to fix persistent right-alignment/centering issues. | Notes: Added .viewport wrapper, changed body overflow/min-height behavior, bounded main, and centered heading lines independently.

content:
Summary: Converted the admin users timestamp from last-login to last-active semantics. The backend now stores and refreshes lastActiveAt whenever an authenticated request resolves a user session, while preserving lastLoginAt as a compatibility fallback for older records. The frontend admin users table and mobile user cards now show Last active instead of Last login.

Facts:
1. [finished] The frontend users admin view now reads and displays ManagedUser.lastActiveAt instead of lastLoginAt.
2. [finished] Authenticated requests refresh the user's active timestamp in backend handler.requireUser, so page/API access updates activity time.
3. [finished] The identity store keeps a compatibility fallback from legacy lastLoginAt data to the new lastActiveAt field when serializing users.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\backend\internal\identity\store.go — Added lastActiveAt persistence, compatibility fallback serialization, and the TouchLastActive storage method used by authenticated access. | Notes: Also kept lastLoginAt as a deprecated alias in the public JSON contract.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\backend\internal\api\handler.go — Refreshed the active timestamp on successful login, password-based session renewal, and every authenticated request that passes requireUser. | Notes: The handler now writes back the current active time into the in-memory user before returning auth responses.
3. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\users\types.ts — Renamed the managed user timestamp contract to lastActiveAt so the admin UI matches the new backend field. | Notes: The frontend type no longer depends on lastLoginAt.
4. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\users\UsersTable.tsx — Updated the admin users table and mobile cards to label and render Last active using the new timestamp field. | Notes: Kept the existing visual layout and added a neutral empty-state message for users with no activity yet.

The backend now stores and refreshes lastActiveAt whenever an authenticated request resolves a user session, while preserving lastLoginAt as a compatibility fallback for older records. The frontend admin users table and mobile user cards now show Last active instead of Last login.

content:
Summary: Added an admin-only account filter to the routes table so admins can filter routes by owner name, while keeping the existing Latest/A-Z sort. The selected owner filter persists in localStorage per signed-in admin and resets safely when invalid or cleared. Route workspace now passes current user identity into the table, and the filter logic was isolated into a small preferences helper.

Facts:
1. [finished] The route table now supports an admin-only account filter that uses route.ownerId and route.ownerName to let admins view routes by account owner.
2. [finished] The selected account filter persists in localStorage using a key scoped by the current user ID, so the choice survives reloads until changed.
3. [finished] The empty-state clear action now resets search, status filter, and account filter together so filtered-empty views can be recovered consistently.
4. [finished] Route workspace passes currentUserId and currentUserName into RouteTable so the filter can label the current account and persist preferences per user.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\RouteTable.tsx — Added admin-only owner filtering, persisted selection wiring, and empty-state clearing behavior in the routes table UI. | Notes: Also now accepts current user identity props from the workspace.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\RoutesWorkspace.tsx — Passed current user identity to the route table so filter persistence can be scoped per signed-in user and account labels can be rendered. | Notes: No backend changes were required.
3. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\routeTablePreferences.ts — Centralized localStorage keying and read/write helpers for the persisted account filter preference. | Notes: Keeps storage access out of the main table component.

The selected owner filter persists in localStorage per signed-in admin and resets safely when invalid or cleared. Route workspace now passes current user identity into the table, and the filter logic was isolated into a small preferences helper.
